findings.finding: Simplest fix: extend build-plugin to rsync top-level hooks/cortex-validate-commit.sh into plugins/cortex-interactive/hooks/, eliminating dual-source condition without a new enforcement mechanism. Plan rejected this under a falsified 'changes rarely' premise.
findings.evidence_quote: Alternative: extend `build-plugin` to template `hooks.json` from a source file. Rejected for now

findings.finding: Task 5 error message tells the user what's wrong but not how to recover. Missing 'cd to repo root and re-invoke' directive.
findings.evidence_quote: /cortex:evolve must be invoked from a cortex-command checkout (missing .git/ or backlog/ in $PWD)

findings.finding: Task 17 smoke test does not specify invocation from a subdirectory — the primary regression surface introduced by Task 5. A smoke run from repo root exercises only the happy path.

Part A/Part B partition leaves 15 bare slash-command references in skills/morning-review/ and skills/overnight/ outside every sweep. No task rewrites them; no verification grep scopes to them. Ticket-121 inherits undocumented coordination debt.
findings.reviewer_angle: Scope hole for non-shipped skills' namespace state
findings.evidence_quote: Task 10 invocation scope: --include skills/commit --include skills/pr ... --include skills/critical-review

findings.finding: Task 15 drift enforcement has no visibility into non-shipped skill tree (morning-review, overnight, skill-creator). Any namespace-state inconsistency in those directories between ticket-120 and ticket-121 is invisible.
findings.reviewer_angle: Scope hole for non-shipped skills' namespace state
findings.evidence_quote: Explicitly excludes `morning-review`, `overnight`, `skill-creator` — those are not in this plugin.
